summarization.a: The line chart illustrates the relationship between temperature and EPC (Å) across different studies, including this work on SF6 plasma and previous studies on thermal ALE with HF/TMA.

a.question: For a temperature-sensitive substrate, which etch route is more suitable: SF₆ plasma/TMA or thermal HF/TM? Why?
a.answer: The plot shows that SF₆ plasma/TMA  gives the highest EPC at a given substrate temperature compared with the HF/TMA studies. That means you can achieve the same total etch depth at a lower temperature than with HF/TMA. Based on this trend alone, SF₆ plasma/TMA is the better choice for temperature-sensitive substrates.

a.question: At approximately which temperature does ALE/ALD switch occur for plasma etching?
Which method (plasma vs thermal etching) achieves the highest etch rate at 250 °C ?
At 300 °C, which studies report an etch per cycle (EPC), and how do their etch rates rank from highest to lowest?
a.answer: Cano (HF/TMA): 2.5 Å/cycle, Hennessy (HF/TMA): 1.2 Å/cycle, Lee (HF/TMA): 0.45 Å/cycle
